me_kan.gloss: to worry (someone); to be worrying, alarming

nya.gloss: surprisingly; oddly enough (discourse marker, e.g. Herannya, dia tidak marah)

me_kan.gloss: to care about; to pay attention to; to heed

nya.gloss: unfortunately; what a pity; sadly (very common discourse marker)

me_i.gloss: to believe in; to be convinced of (something)

me_kan.gloss: doubtful; questionable; to doubt/cast doubt on (something)

an.gloss: surprise (noun), e.g. kejutan = surprise/pesta kejutan = surprise party

me_i.gloss: to shower (someone) with (something), figurative (menghujani pertanyaan = to bombard with questions)

ke_an.gloss: to get caught in the rain; rained on

nya.gloss: the rain; how much it's raining

me_kan.gloss: to air out (something); to let air through; to ventilate

me.gloss: to rise into the sky/clouds; to soar (literary)
to pile up (like a mountain); to accumulate greatly (figurative, e.g. utang menggunung = debt piled up)

se.gloss: as big as a mountain (figurative, e.g. masalahnya segunung)

me.gloss: to go out to sea (especially to fish)

an.gloss: ocean; vast sea (emphatic/larger form, e.g. lautan manusia = a sea of people)

me_kan.gloss: to reforest; to turn (land) into forest
to ostracize; to boycott; to shun (someone) -- figurative, like isolating someone on an island

per_an.gloss: rural areas (formal/administrative register; "pedesaan" without the r is more common colloquially)

me.gloss: to skyrocket; to soar (prices, fame, etc., figurative)

se.gloss: sky-high (e.g. harganya selangit = the price is sky-high)

me.gloss: to become grounded/down-to-earth; to take root (figurative, of an idea spreading)

me_kan.gloss: to ground (something); to make (an idea) accessible/relatable

me.gloss: to flood; to pour in (literally or figuratively)

me_i.gloss: to flood/inundate (something) with, often figurative (barang impor membanjiri pasar)

ke_an.gloss: to be flooded; flooded with, also figurative (kebanjiran tugas = flooded with assignments)

me.gloss: to smooth a surface by rubbing (cloth, wood); to remove wrinkles or roughen

pe.gloss: rinsing agent; something that rinses (e.g. air pembilas, rinse aid)

pe_an.gloss: the act or process of rinsing

ber.gloss: to rinse off with clean water after bathing/swimming (e.g. in the sea or a pool), as a second wash
to have a head (figurative), e.g. berkepala dua = two-headed, duplicitous

me_i.gloss: to handle; to deal with; to take care of

ber.gloss: to have hands (figurative), e.g. bertangan kosong = empty-handed

pe.notes: "penangan" only really appears inside "penanganan"; not used as a standalone word

me_i.gloss: to skin, flay; to peel off the skin/rind of

ber.gloss: to have skin of a certain type/color, e.g. berkulit putih = fair-skinned
to have a brain/mind; to be intelligent, e.g. berotak cerdas = brainy
to have hair (of a certain length/type), e.g. berambut panjang = long-haired
to have eyes of a certain color/type, e.g. bermata biru = blue-eyed

me_i.notes: the "to spy on" sense is the reduplicated "memata-matai" (from mata-mata, spy); reduplication isn't modeled by this affix system

ber.gloss: to have a face (figurative), e.g. bermuka dua = two-faced, hypocritical
to have a belly of a certain type, e.g. berperut buncit = potbellied
to have fingers, e.g. berjari lima = five-fingered
to have ears (figurative), e.g. bertelinga tajam = sharp-eared, perceptive
to have a neck of a certain type, e.g. berleher jenjang = long-necked (elegant)
to have a chest of a certain type, e.g. berdada bidang = broad-chested

me_kan.notes: the real verb "memperhatikan" (to pay attention to) is the compound mem-per-...-kan; no single affix id in data/affixes.json models a stacked per-+kan circumfix, so this can't be derived or overridden correctly

kan.notes: collision triage (#48): mechanical anakan collides with anak + -an; ana (slang pronoun) does not take -kan

ber_an.notes: collision triage (#48): mechanical bertangan collides with tangan + ber-; tang (pliers) has no ber-...-an form

me_kan.notes: collision triage (#48): menangkan (win it!) is menang + kan; tang (pliers) has no me-...-kan verb form (nasal assimilation coincidentally produces the same string as menang)

ber.notes: collision triage (#48): berubah is from ubah; rubah (fox) has no ber- verb (the colloquial merubah spelling also derives from ubah, not fox)

ter.notes: collision triage (#48): terubah is from ubah (r-elision on rubah gives the same string); rubah (fox) has no ter- form

an.notes: collision triage (#48): real ketuaan is ke- + tua + -an ('too old'); ketua + -an is not a word

me_i.notes: collision triage (#48): memarahi is from marah; worsening something is memperparah (per- causative, not modeled by this affix set)

me_kan.notes: collision triage (#48): memarahkan is from marah; worsening something is memperparah (per- causative, not modeled by this affix set)

pe.notes: collision triage (#48): pemarah (hot-tempered person) is from marah, not parah

me_i.notes: collision triage (#48): membeli is from beli; ringing a bell is membunyikan bel, membeli from bel is not a word

me_kan.notes: collision triage (#48): mengasarkan is from kasar; asar (the afternoon prayer) takes no verb forms

me_i.notes: collision triage (#48): mengotaki is from otak (to mastermind); the kotak verb is the reduplicated mengotak-ngotakkan, which this affix set does not model

me.notes: collision triage (#48): mengubah is from ubah; there is no verb 'to dome' from kubah

me_kan.notes: collision triage (#48): mengubahkan is from ubah; there is no verb 'to dome' from kubah

pe.notes: collision triage (#48): pengubah (one who changes) is from ubah, not kubah

pe_an.notes: collision triage (#48): pengubahan (alteration) is from ubah, not kubah

me.notes: collision triage (#48): menipis is from tipis; nipis is a Malay/regional variant of tipis (as in jeruk nipis) and does not take Indonesian verb affixes on its own

me_kan.notes: collision triage (#48): menipiskan is from tipis; nipis is a Malay/regional variant of tipis and does not take Indonesian verb affixes on its own

an.notes: collision triage (#48): seringan is se- + ringan ('as light as'); the colloquial comparative sering-an ('more often') is nonstandard Jakarta speech

kan.notes: collision triage (#48): memulai is itself a derived form (me- + mulai) sitting in words.json as a root; double-affixing it duplicates mulai + me-...-kan
collision triage (#48): menari is itself a derived form (me- + tari); double-affixing it duplicates tari + me-...-kan

an.notes: collision triage (#48): menarik is itself a derived form (me- + tarik); menarikan is tari + me-...-kan, not menarik + -an

i.notes: collision triage (#48/#56): menarik is itself me- + tarik sitting in words.json as a root (#52); menariki is tarik + me-...-i

an.notes: collision triage (#48): menyamak is itself a derived form (me- + samak); the tanning noun is penyamakan, not menyamak + -an

kan.notes: collision triage (#48): menyanyi is itself a derived form (me- + nyanyi); double-affixing it duplicates nyanyi + me-...-kan

an.notes: collision triage (#48): peminum is itself a derived form (pe- + minum); peminuman duplicates minum + pe-...-an

me.notes: collision triage (#48): KBBI derives meminta from pinta as well as minta; both roots are legitimate

me_i.notes: collision triage (#48): parallel to minta + me-...-i; both roots are legitimate

me_kan.notes: collision triage (#48): parallel to minta + me-...-kan; both roots are legitimate

pe.notes: collision triage (#48): parallel to minta + pe-; both roots are legitimate

nya.notes: collision triage (#48): -nya is fully productive on nouns; kept valid because penanya is a genuine homograph with tanya + pe- (questioner)
